operation: First, an XY workplane is initialized at an offset of 13 units along the positive Z axis. A closed 2D profile is sketched on this workplane: starting at coordinate (-60, 0), draw a 3 unit line along positive Y, a 25 unit line along positive X, a sloped line with 10 unit positive X and 7 unit positive Y offset, a 50 unit line along positive X, a sloped line with 10 unit positive X and 7 unit negative Y offset, a 25 unit line along positive X, a 3 unit line along negative Y, then close the profile loop. The closed 2D profile is revolved 360 degrees around the X-axis to produce the final 3D solid.
geometry: This is a fully axisymmetric solid of revolution aligned around a single central longitudinal horizontal axis. It features two identical small-diameter cylindrical shafts protruding outward from opposite ends, each connected via a tapered conical frustum transition section to a larger-diameter straight cylindrical main body positioned at the center of the part.
function: This is a symmetrical rotary mechanical component, intended for use as a roller, drive shaft segment, or precision mounting pin, designed to be supported at its two small end shafts while the larger central section interfaces with mating mechanical parts.
